subject: Astronomy & Space
title: T Tauri Stars: Stellar Toddlers
description: Learn T Tauri Stars: Stellar Toddlers in one minute.

hook.text: T Tauri stars are young stars still settling down. They flare wildly and vary in brightness over days.

buildup.text: They're surrounded by disks of gas and dust—the raw material that will form planets, moons, and asteroids.

discovery.text: Our Sun was a T Tauri star 4.6 billion years ago. The solar system's planets formed from its leftover disk.

twist.text: T Tauri stars produce powerful jets of material that shoot thousands of km/s into space from their poles.

climax.text: Studying T Tauri stars lets us rewind time and understand how our own solar system was born.

punchline.text: Tantrum-throwing baby stars. Our Sun was once one of them.

quiz.question: What surrounds T Tauri stars that is significant for planet formation?
options: Disks of gas and dust that become planets, moons, and asteroids
Rings of ice crystals that reflect starlight
Clouds of antimatter from early fusion reactions
